data.title: Is It Time To Reassess SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) After Its 203% One Year Surge?
data.description:
• Wondering whether SolarEdge Technologies at US$37.83 still reflects its true worth, or if the stock price has moved ahead of, or behind, the underlying business.
  • The share price has had a mixed run, with a 13.1% decline over the last 7 days and a 7.1% decline over the last 30 days. Yet it is up 20.6% year to date and 202.9% over the past year, while longer term 3 year and 5 year returns of 88.0% and 85.2% declines tell a very different story.
  • Recent coverage has focused on SolarEdge as a key name in solar and power electronics, with headlines often circling around its position within the wider semiconductor and clean energy space. This attention helps frame recent price moves as investors reassess both the risks and potential rewards tied to that role.
  • Simply Wall St’s valuation framework currently gives SolarEdge a 3 out of 6 value score. The next sections will break down traditional valuation tools like DCFs and multiples, before finishing with a broader way to think about what the current price really implies.

SolarEdge Technologies delivered 202.9% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Semiconductor industry.

Approach 1: SolarEdge Technologies Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes projected future cash flows and discounts them back to today to estimate what the entire business could be worth in present value terms.

For SolarEdge Technologies, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow sits at about $28.75 million. Analysts provide explicit forecasts for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extends those estimates further. Within these projections, free cash flow is expected to reach $195 million in 2030, with intermediate years such as 2026, 2027 and 2028 also modeled in the $100 million to $200 million range, all expressed in dollars.

When these projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $21.54 per share. Compared with the current share price of $37.83, this framework suggests the stock is 75.6% above the DCF-based estimate of intrinsic value.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests SolarEdge Technologies may be overvalued by 75.6%. Discover 60 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for SolarEdge Technologies.

Approach 2: SolarEdge Technologies Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings are volatile or negative, the P/S ratio is often a useful way to think about valuation, because it compares what you pay for each dollar of revenue without relying on near term profits.

Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/S ratio, while slower expected growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower, more conservative multiple. So context really matters when you compare any single number.

SolarEdge Technologies currently trades on a P/S ratio of 1.93x. That sits well below the broader Semiconductor industry average P/S of 6.17x and also below the peer group average of 15.00x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework estimates a P/S of 2.44x for SolarEdge, based on factors such as its earnings growth profile, profit margins, size, risk characteristics and industry.

This Fair Ratio is designed to be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the industry, because it adjusts for the company’s specific growth outlook, risk, profitability, sector and market cap rather than assuming all semiconductor names should trade on the same multiple.

Comparing the current 1.93x P/S with the Fair Ratio of 2.44x suggests the shares are trading below that modelled level.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Narratives are that tool, letting you attach your own story about SolarEdge Technologies to the numbers by linking a view on future revenue, earnings and margins to a Fair Value that can be compared with the current price. All of this happens within Simply Wall St’s Community page where Narratives update automatically as new earnings or news arrive. For SolarEdge, you might see one Narrative that anchors on a Fair Value around US$26.20 with more cautious assumptions, and another closer to US$46.88 with more optimistic expectations. By choosing which story feels more realistic, you can quickly see whether the current share price of US$37.83 sits above or below your chosen Fair Value and use that as a structured input when considering your investment decisions.

For SolarEdge Technologies, however, we'll make it really easy for you with previews of two leading SolarEdge Technologies Narratives:

Fair value: US$46.88 per share

  • Sees growth potential from Nexis and broader integrated offerings across PV, storage, EV charging and energy management, with recurring software and service revenue playing a bigger role.

🐻 SolarEdge Technologies Bear Case

data.description:

Operating results spark fresh focus on Norwegian Air Shuttle

Norwegian Air Shuttle (OB:NAS) has drawn fresh attention after reporting March 2026 operating figures, with higher load factors and passenger volumes for both the month and the twelve month rolling period.

Available seat kilometers for March were 2,610 million compared with 2,770 million a year earlier, while revenue passenger kilometers were 2,290 million versus 2,244 million. The monthly load factor reached 87.7% compared with 81.0%, supported by 1,677,290 passengers versus 1,586,532 last year.

On a twelve month rolling basis, ASK stood at 37,224 million compared with 37,554 million, and RPK at 32,315 million versus 31,739 million. The load factor was 86.8% compared with 84.5%, with 23,205,994 passengers versus 22,815,147 in the prior period.

  • ⚠️ If the retail voting program continues to lean toward board recommendations, smaller shareholders may feel their influence is limited, which can affect how engaged they remain with the stock during periods of stress.
  • 🎁 A more contested voting process can surface a wider range of views on topics like energy transition, capital allocation and Guyana exposure. This can give you more information for comparing Exxon to peers such as Chevron, Shell and BP.
  • 🎁 Clear disclosure of proposals and management’s stance allows investors to decide whether Exxon's current power structure matches their preferences on board oversight and long-term risk control.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch how much support each governance proposal receives at the 27 May 2026 annual meeting and whether Exxon adjusts its retail voting program in response. Pay attention to how large pension funds and long-term holders explain their votes, since that can hint at how they view Exxon's risk profile relative to other integrated oil majors. It is also worth tracking whether future proxy statements continue to attract similar proposals or if this year marks a turning point in board and shareholder engagement.
